description: Envelope resource-bound rejections under the reference bounds MAX_SLOTS = 1024 and a 65536-byte decoded-envelope ceiling, both named in validator_options. These records exceed the Cardano maxTxSize and could never appear on chain; an implementation validating caller-supplied bytes MUST still pin the rejection, before any KEM or AEAD primitive runs.

vectors.name: enc-slots-1025
vectors.note: 1025 distinct x25519 slots exceed the reference MAX_SLOTS = 1024. Under the reference bounds the two resource codes co-fire: 1025 classical slots cannot encode below the 65536-byte envelope bound (the decoded envelope here is 96489 bytes). Both bounds are deployment-pinned; the expectation assumes the stated reference values.

vectors.name: enc-envelope-over-65536-bytes
vectors.note: 56 distinct hybrid slots (within MAX_SLOTS) decode to a 66562-byte envelope, exceeding the reference 65536-byte envelope bound; only the size code fires. The bound is deployment-pinned; the expectation assumes the stated reference value.
